name: Home
description: Start of the epic journey
highlights: Pack your America the Beautiful pass ($80/yr covers all parks)
Sort all permits before you leave — Half Dome and Havasu sell out months out
Service your vehicle — oil, tires, brakes

name: Acadia NP
description: Precipice Trail · Cadillac Sunrise · Sand Beach · Thunder Hole
highlights: Precipice Trail — iron rungs on a sheer cliff face, thrilling
Cadillac Mountain sunrise — first sunrise in the US (vehicle reservation required)
Thunder Hole at high tide for dramatic waves
Jordan Pond House for popovers — classic tradition
Stewman's Lobster Pound in Bar Harbor — best lobster meal on the trip

name: Mt. Washington
description: 6,288 ft — highest peak in the Northeast
highlights: Tuckerman Ravine Trail — most popular hike, 4 miles up
Auto Road drive to the summit if not hiking
Cog Railway — oldest mountain-climbing cog railway in the world
Alpine zone above treeline — unique fragile ecosystem
Mount Washington Observatory at summit — weather station

name: Adirondacks / Saranac Lake
description: Kayaking · fire tower hikes · 46 High Peaks
highlights: Kayak Saranac Lake chain — multi-day route possible
Saint Regis Canoe Area for wilderness paddling
Fire tower hike (Kane Mountain or Azure Mountain)
Saranac Lake village — charming, great food

name: Boldt Castle & Thousand Islands
description: Castle boat tour · St. Lawrence River · 1,000 Islands
highlights: Boldt Castle boat tour from Alexandria Bay
Rent a skiff for island hopping
Briefly cross into Canada for a different vantage point

name: Rochester
description: Visit Sam, Carter, DiMartino + Strong Museum of Play
highlights: Strong National Museum of Play — massive, legitimately fun
Garbage Plate at Nick Tahou's — a Rochester institution

name: Chicago / Madison
description: Architecture river cruise · Visit Jan Lohs & Cousin Dick
highlights: Chicago Architecture Foundation river cruise — absolutely do this
Millennium Park and The Bean
Visit Jan Lohs and Cousin Dick in Madison
UW Memorial Union terrace on Lake Mendota
Deep dish at Lou Malnati's

name: Badlands NP
description: Castle Trailhead · Prairie Homestead · Cedar Pass Lodge
highlights: Castle Trail — 10-mile best hike in the park
Shoot at golden hour — the light is otherworldly
Roberts Prairie Dog Town
Wounded Knee Museum — important and sobering

name: Mount Rushmore
description: Presidential Trail loop · Sculptor's Studio
highlights: Presidential Trail for best close-up views
Sculptor's Studio shows original models
Evening lighting ceremony in summer

name: Crazy Horse Memorial
description: Largest mountain carving in the world — still in progress
highlights: Still being carved solo since 1969 — Korczak's ongoing vision
Scale is staggering — Rushmore's Washington fits in one nostril
Lakota cultural exhibits

name: Deadwood / Dinosaur Park
description: Wild West history · Custer State Park · hilltop concrete dinos
highlights: Dinosaur Park — hilltop concrete dinos, free, great views over Rapid City
Deadwood historic district — Wild Bill Hickok territory
Saloon No. 10 where Wild Bill was shot
Custer State Park bison herd wildlife loop

name: Devil's Tower
description: Sacred Lakota site · 867-ft monolith · wildlife loop
highlights: Tower Trail loop (1.3 miles around the base — do it)
Best at sunrise when light hits the columns
Respect the prayer bundles — sacred Lakota site
Rock climbers visible on columns most summer days

name: Theodore Roosevelt NP
description: Painted Canyon · bison herds · badlands loop
highlights: Painted Canyon overlook — first view is jaw-dropping
Scenic Loop Drive (36 miles, South Unit) — bison often block road
North Unit is quieter and more dramatic
Petrified Forest Trail (off-road, get a map)

name: Yellowstone NP
description: Grand Prismatic · Old Faithful · Lamar Valley · Boiling River
highlights: Grand Prismatic Spring — view from overlook trail above, not the boardwalk
Lamar Valley at dawn for wolves and bison — bring binoculars
Boiling River hot spring swim (seasonal)
Grand Canyon of Yellowstone Upper + Lower Falls
Mammoth Hot Springs terraces
Smith Mansion — quirky roadside stop near Cody

name: Grand Teton NP
description: Mormon Row · TA Moulton Barn · Jenny Lake · Cascade Canyon
highlights: TA Moulton Barn at Mormon Row — most photographed barn in America, sunrise
Jenny Lake boat shuttle + Cascade Canyon hike (non-negotiable)
Antler Arch Square in Jackson town
String Lake for a crowd-free swim

name: Flathead Lake
description: US-93 lakeshore drive · St. Ignatius Mission · kayaking
highlights: Drive US-93 north along the lake — one of Montana's most scenic stretches, lake on one side, Mission Mountains on the other
St. Ignatius Mission — historic 1891 Jesuit mission with 58 hand-painted ceiling and wall murals, framed against the Mission Range
Wild Horse Island State Park — accessible by boat, wild horses + bighorn sheep
Cherry season in July — roadside stands on US-93
Polson Flathead Lake Brewing Co.

name: Glacier NP
description: Going-to-the-Sun Road · Grinnell Lake · Highline Trail · Dark Sky
highlights: Going-to-the-Sun Road — 50-mile drive across the Continental Divide
Highline Trail from Logan Pass — 2,000 ft, 12 miles, the best hike in the park
Grinnell Lake — boat tour from Many Glacier Hotel or full hike
Ptarmigan Tunnel — seasonal, walk through rock into a hidden valley
Wild Goose Island at sunrise
Dark Sky park — Milky Way is real here
Waterton Lakes NP just across the Canadian border

name: Banff NP
description: Moraine Lake · Lake Louise · Peyto Lake · Emerald Lake
highlights: Moraine Lake — arrive before 6am or take the mandatory shuttle (parking full instantly)
Lake Louise — iconic, crowded, still essential
Peyto Lake at Bow Summit — less crowded, equally stunning turquoise wolf-shaped lake
Emerald Lake in adjacent Yoho NP
Johnston Canyon lower and upper falls
Banff Upper Hot Springs soak

name: Yoho NP
description: Takakkaw Falls · Emerald Lake · Natural Bridge
highlights: Takakkaw Falls — second highest waterfall in Canada, short walk
Emerald Lake circuit (5km) — stunning
Natural Bridge over Kicking Horse River — roadside stop
Spiral Tunnels viewpoint — watch freight trains loop through the mountains

name: Vancouver
description: Britannia Mine Museum · Garibaldi Park · Shannon Falls
highlights: Britannia Mine Museum north on Sea-to-Sky Hwy
Shannon Falls Provincial Park — third highest waterfall in BC
Garibaldi Provincial Park for serious alpine hiking
Stanley Park seawall bike or walk
Granville Island Public Market

name: San Juan Island
description: Orca watching · Lime Kiln State Park · Friday Harbor
highlights: Lime Kiln State Park — best land-based orca watching spot in the world
Friday Harbor waterfront food and wandering
Kayak tour — sea otters and harbor seals common

name: Olympic NP
description: Hoh Rainforest · Hall of Mosses · Ruby Beach · Cape Flattery
highlights: Hall of Mosses Trail in Hoh Rainforest — otherworldly
Ruby Beach — driftwood, sea stacks, tide pools
Cape Flattery — northwesternmost point in the contiguous US
Shi Shi Beach backpack (permit required) — best beach in WA
Hurricane Ridge alpine views

name: Seattle
description: Pike Place · MoPOP · Space Needle · Columbia Gorge day trip
highlights: Pike Place Market (original Starbucks, fish throw)
Museum of Pop Culture (MoPOP) — genuinely great
Capitol Hill food and nightlife
Columbia River Gorge day trip — Multnomah Falls 2.5 hrs south

name: Mount Rainier NP
description: Paradise wildflowers · Skyline Trail · Grove of the Patriarchs
highlights: Paradise area — best wildflower meadows in the Cascades (July/August)
Skyline Trail Loop from Paradise — 5.5 miles, summit views
Grove of the Patriarchs — 1,000-year-old trees
Silver Falls Trail from Ohanapecosh — underrated loop

name: North Cascades NP
description: Highway 20 · Diablo Lake · Washington Pass Overlook
highlights: Washington Pass Overlook — Liberty Bell and Early Winter Spires (jaw-dropping)
Diablo Lake turquoise viewpoint
Desolation Peak Trail — Jack Kerouac wrote Big Sur from the lookout here

name: Mount St. Helens
description: Summit climb (permit req) · Johnston Ridge · Ape Cave
highlights: Monitor Ridge summit — 17 miles RT, non-technical but very strenuous
Johnston Ridge Observatory — best views into the active crater
Ape Cave lava tube — bring two flashlights per person
Lava Canyon Trail — unique volcanic landscape

name: Portland
description: Japanese Garden · Powell's Books · Columbia Gorge day trip
highlights: Multnomah Falls in Columbia River Gorge (~$2 timed entry at recreation.gov)
Portland Japanese Garden — among the best outside Japan
Powell's Books — legendary independent bookstore
Food carts — Portland invented this scene

name: Oregon Coast
description: Haystack Rock · Thor's Well · Heceta Head Lighthouse
highlights: Haystack Rock tide pools with tufted puffins in season
Thor's Well at Cape Perpetua — best at high tide
Heceta Head Lighthouse — most photographed on the Oregon Coast
Samuel H. Boardman Scenic Corridor (south coast) for best scenery

name: Crater Lake NP
description: Rim Drive · Wizard Island · Garfield Peak · deepest lake in the US
highlights: Rim Drive (33-mile loop) — 8 viewpoints, every one worth stopping
Cleetwood Cove Trail — only legal lake access, bring a swimsuit
Wizard Island boat tour — reserve early
Garfield Peak Trail — best rim hike

name: Lassen Volcanic NP
description: Bumpass Hell · 4 volcano types in one park (added stop)
highlights: Bumpass Hell — largest hydrothermal area in the park (boardwalk)
Lassen Peak summit (2.5 miles, 2,000 ft) — views of 4 states
Cinder Cone — perfectly shaped volcanic cone with painted dunes
Kings Creek Falls — easy 2.5 miles

name: Lake Tahoe
description: Emerald Bay · Vikingsholm · D.L. Bliss · crystal clear water
highlights: Emerald Bay — most photographed spot in California
Vikingsholm Castle (short hike down to the bay)
D.L. Bliss State Park for swimming
Sand Harbor Beach (Nevada side) — crystal clear water

name: Mendocino / Fort Bragg
description: Glass Beach · Headlands · Skunk Train through redwoods
highlights: Glass Beach — decades of sea-tumbled glass, very photogenic
Mendocino Headlands walk — coastal bluffs above crashing waves
Skunk Train through the redwoods from Fort Bragg
Noyo Harbor for fresh Dungeness crab

name: San Francisco Metro
description: Bradshaw & Akhil · Sonoma Raceway · Apple Campus · Palo Alto
highlights: Visit Bradshaw & Akhil — networking for Alphalog
Sonoma Raceway and Laguna Seca
Apple Campus in Cupertino
Golden Gate Bridge walk at sunrise
Muir Woods redwoods (timed entry at gomuirwoods.com)
Ferry Building Saturday farmers market

name: Santa Cruz
description: Boardwalk · Natural Bridges · Henry Cowell Redwoods
highlights: Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk
Natural Bridges State Beach — monarch butterfly migration
Henry Cowell Redwoods old growth forest

name: Monterey & Big Sur
description: Aquarium · 17-Mile Drive · Carmel · Big Sur Hwy 1
highlights: Monterey Bay Aquarium — world-class, half-day minimum
17-Mile Drive through Pebble Beach — $12 toll, worth it
Big Sur Hwy 1 south — McWay Falls, Pfeiffer Beach, purple sand
Point Lobos State Reserve for sea otters and seals

name: Yosemite NP
description: Half Dome · Clouds Rest · Glacier Point · El Cap · Mist Trail
highlights: Half Dome — permit required for cables section, 17 miles RT, 4,800 ft gain
Clouds Rest — better views than Half Dome, less permit competition
Glacier Point sunset (drive or hike up)
Mist Trail — Vernal and Nevada Falls, very wet and beautiful
El Capitan meadow at dawn — watch climbers on the wall
Ansel Adams Gallery photo tours
Mariposa Grove Giant Sequoias

name: Sequoia NP
description: General Sherman Tree · Moro Rock · Crystal Cave
highlights: General Sherman Tree — largest living thing on Earth by volume
Moro Rock — 350-step staircase to panoramic views, required stop
Crystal Cave tour (reserve at recreation.gov in advance)
Tokopah Falls — easy 4-mile RT to 1,200-ft waterfall

name: Kings Canyon NP
description: Kings Canyon Scenic Byway · Roaring River Falls · General Grant Grove · Whitney Portal Rd detour
highlights: Kings Canyon Scenic Byway — most dramatic road descent in the US
Roaring River Falls — short walk to powerful falls
General Grant Grove — second largest tree on Earth
Zumwalt Meadows loop — wildflowers and canyon views

Whitney Portal Road detour (en route to Death Valley): exit the Sierra, drop south on US-395 to Lone Pine, then drive 13 miles of switchbacks up Whitney Portal Rd to the Mt. Whitney trailhead at 8,360 ft — towering granite walls of the highest peak in the Lower 48 right above you
miles: 235 mi → Death Valley via Lone Pine

name: Death Valley NP
description: Badwater Basin · Dante's View · Racetrack Playa · Eureka Dunes
highlights: Badwater Basin — 282 ft below sea level, largest US salt flat
Dante's View — 5,476 ft overlook over the entire valley
Mesquite Flat Sand Dunes at sunrise
Racetrack Playa moving stones (4WD required)
Zabriskie Point — iconic badlands
Artist's Drive multi-colored volcanic deposits

name: Fly Geyser
description: Rainbow-colored accidental geothermal geyser
highlights: Created accidentally by a well drill in 1964
Rainbow colors from thermophilic algae — looks alien
Tour only via Burning Man LLC (flygeyser.org)
Stunning photography at golden hour

name: Burning Man
description: Late August / early September · tickets.burningman.org
highlights: Tickets via lottery at tickets.burningman.org — apply in January
Bring a bike — the city is 2 miles across
Radical self-reliance: bring all food, water, shade
Deep playa art installations at 3am — otherworldly
The Man burns Saturday night, The Temple Sunday

name: Las Vegas
description: Sphere · Blackjack · Poker · Red Rock Canyon · Hoover Dam
highlights: See a show at the Sphere (thespherevegas.com)
Win a hand of Blackjack and a few rounds of Texas hold'em
Red Rock Canyon NCA — 30 min west, free entry
Hoover Dam — go early before tour buses
Valley of Fire State Park — 40 min north, stunning red rocks

name: Havasu Falls
description: Turquoise waterfalls in the Grand Canyon · permit required
highlights: Havasu Falls — 100-ft drop into turquoise pool
Mooney Falls — more dramatic, requires chains and ladder descent
Beaver Falls — 3 more miles downstream, worth it
Navajo Falls and Fifty Foot Falls also along the creek
Supai Village — only US village still served by mule mail

name: Grand Canyon NP
description: South Kaibab · Bright Angel · Grandview Trail · Helicopter tour
highlights: South Kaibab to Ooh Aah Point (easy) or Skeleton Point (moderate)
Bright Angel Trail — most popular rim-to-river
Grandview Trail — steeper, fewer crowds, Horseshoe Mesa
Papillon Helicopter Tour — worth the splurge for perspective
Desert View Watchtower (Mary Colter design)
Hermit Road shuttle for western rim viewpoints

name: Sedona
description: Cathedral Rock · Devil's Bridge · Airport Mesa sunset
highlights: Cathedral Rock at sunset from Red Rock Crossing — most photographed spot
Devil's Bridge Trail — 4.2 miles RT, natural arch
Airport Mesa sunset vortex — accessible viewpoint
Bell Rock and Courthouse Butte loop — easy, iconic

name: Monument Valley
description: The Mittens · John Ford's Point · sunrise photography
highlights: Valley Drive (17-mile dirt loop) — doable in a standard car when dry
Sunrise from The View Hotel viewpoint — legendary
John Ford's Point — classic Western movie location
Wildcat Trail (3.2 miles) — only self-guided hike

name: Four Corners Monument
description: Only place 4 states meet · Navajo Nation · quick stop
highlights: Stand in 4 states simultaneously
Navajo vendors selling jewelry and fry bread
Quick stop — 20 minutes is plenty

name: Mesa Verde NP
description: Cliff Palace · Balcony House · Long House (ranger tours)
highlights: Cliff Palace — largest cliff dwelling in North America (ranger tour required)
Balcony House — more adventurous, ladders and a tunnel
Long House on Wetherill Mesa — less crowded option
Chapin Mesa Archeological Museum for context

name: Antelope Canyon & Horseshoe Bend
description: Navajo slot canyon · Horseshoe Bend · Page, AZ
highlights: Upper Antelope Canyon "The Crack" — must be on a Navajo tour
Lower Antelope Canyon "The Corkscrew" — narrower, more adventurous
Horseshoe Bend — 180° bend of the Colorado River, $10 parking
Book Upper Canyon tours months in advance for morning light beams

name: Zion NP
description: Angels Landing · The Narrows · Zion–Mt. Carmel Hwy · The Subway
highlights: Zion–Mt. Carmel Highway (SR-9) — drive the engineering-marvel switchbacks up the east side and through the 1.1-mile 1930 tunnel; emerge at slickrock canyon country and Checkerboard Mesa. One of the great park drives in America.
Angels Landing — permit required, chains section, 1,488 ft gain in 2.5 miles
The Narrows bottom-up — wade through the Virgin River (no permit needed)
The Subway — technical canyoneering, permit required
Observation Point — better views than Angels Landing, no permit
Weeping Rock — short walk to hanging garden
Grafton Ghost Town — 4 miles from park, photogenic 1800s ruins

name: Bryce Canyon NP
description: Sunrise Point · Queens Garden · Wall Street · Thor's Hammer
highlights: Sunrise at Sunrise Point — hoodoos glow orange-red
Queens Garden Trail — easiest canyon hike, highly recommended
Navajo Loop via Wall Street — best hoodoo immersion
Scenic Byway 12 heading east — one of the most scenic drives in the US

name: Capitol Reef NP
description: Waterpocket Fold · Hickman Bridge · scenic drive · orchards
highlights: Hickman Bridge Trail — 2 miles RT to natural bridge
Scenic Drive through the park (free, 8 miles) — stunning geology
Fruita Historic District — pick fruit in season
Grand Wash canyon for slot canyon feel without permits

name: Canyonlands NP
description: Mesa Arch · White Rim Road · The Maze · Needles District
highlights: Mesa Arch at sunrise — most photographed arch in Utah (arrive 45 min early)
White Rim Road — 100-mile 4WD loop, overnight permit required
The Maze — most remote terrain in any US national park
Needles District — red and white banded towers, less visited

name: Arches NP
description: Delicate Arch · Landscape Arch · Double Arch · Moab
highlights: Delicate Arch — 3 miles RT, 480 ft gain, the icon of Utah. Sunrise or sunset.
Landscape Arch — longest natural arch in the world
Double Arch — walk under and between both arches
Devils Garden Trail — chain of arches, extend to 7 miles with scrambling
Moab: Hell's Revenge offroad, Colorado River rafting, great food

name: Aspen / Maroon Bells
description: Most photographed mountains in North America · 14er climbs
highlights: Maroon Bells — arrive before 8am or after 5pm to drive in for free
Crater Lake Trail from Maroon Lake (3.6 miles RT, moderate)
14er climbs: Maroon Peak, Capitol Peak nearby
Aspen town for world-class food and drinks

name: Great Sand Dunes NP
description: Tallest dunes in North America · sand boarding · UFO Watchtower
highlights: Sand boarding and sledding (boards for rent in Alamosa)
Medano Creek in spring — flows along dune base for wading
High Dune hike — 2.5 miles, 700 ft gain
UFO Watchtower near Hooper — 30 min away, genuinely fun
Exceptional dark skies — bring a tripod

name: Bishop Castle
description: One man built a medieval castle alone for 50+ years
highlights: Jim Bishop has been building this entirely alone since 1969
You can climb to the top — the iron bridge sways
He's often on-site yelling at visitors or the government (part of the experience)
Free to visit, donations appreciated

name: Royal Gorge & Pikes Peak
description: Highest suspension bridge · Cog Railway · 14,115 ft summit
highlights: Royal Gorge Bridge — 955 ft above the Arkansas River
Pikes Peak Cog Railway — reserve at cograilway.com
Drive Pikes Peak Highway to the summit (14,115 ft)
Donuts at the top from the famous summit shop

name: Garden of the Gods
description: Free entry · Balanced Rock · surreal red sandstone formations
highlights: Garden of the Gods — free entry, astonishing red sandstone
Balanced Rock — one of the most precarious in the US
Central Garden Trail loop (1.5 miles — do it)
Colorado Springs for food and resupply

name: Denver / Rocky Mountain NP
description: RMNP · Red Rocks concert · Vail / Breckenridge · Boulder
highlights: Rocky Mountain NP: Trail Ridge Road (highest continuous paved road in US)
Alberta Falls (easy, gorgeous) and Tyndall Glacier
Longs Peak — 14,259 ft, most climbed 14er
Red Rocks Amphitheatre for a concert
Vail or Breckenridge for an alpine town day
Boulder Pearl Street Mall and Chautauqua Park
hrs: ~13.5 hrs (overnight in Santa Fe or Amarillo)

name: Austin
description: Live music capital · BBQ · Lady Bird Lake · 6th Street
highlights: Franklin BBQ or La Barbecue — best brisket on the trip (queue early or use the virtual line)
Live music on 6th Street, Rainey Street, and the Continental Club on South Congress
Lady Bird Lake kayak or paddleboard — downtown skyline views, rentals at Rowing Dock
Mount Bonnell at sunset — highest point in Austin over the Colorado River
Barton Springs Pool — 68° spring-fed swim, year-round
SoCo (South Congress) — vintage shops, food trucks, "I Love You So Much" mural
Bat flight from Congress Avenue Bridge at dusk (Mar–Oct, 1.5M Mexican free-tails)
hrs: ~13 hrs (overnight in Little Rock or Memphis)

name: Nashville
description: Music City · Honky Tonks · Grand Ole Opry · hot chicken
highlights: Honky Tonk Highway on Lower Broadway — live music in every bar, every night
Grand Ole Opry — book at opry.com (Tue/Fri/Sat shows; mainstays at the Ryman in winter)
Ryman Auditorium — "Mother Church of Country Music," tour or catch a show
Country Music Hall of Fame — surprisingly excellent even if country isn't your thing
Hattie B's or Prince's for Nashville hot chicken (start mild — "hot" is no joke)
Bluebird Cafe in Green Hills — songwriters in the round; reserve weeks ahead
RCA Studio B tour — where Elvis cut 200+ songs
Stop in Memphis on the way (Graceland + Sun Studio) if splitting the drive there

name: Louisville
description: Visit Barb · Louisville Slugger Museum · Bourbon Trail
highlights: Visit Barb
Louisville Slugger Museum — watch a bat being made
Churchill Downs and Kentucky Derby Museum
Kentucky Bourbon Trail — Maker's Mark, Buffalo Trace, Woodford Reserve
Muhammad Ali Center in his hometown

name: Tail of the Dragon
description: 318 curves in 11 miles · Deals Gap · Tree of Shame
highlights: US 129 at Deals Gap — 11 miles of pure driving joy
Tree of Shame — motorcycles wrapped around a tree at the bottom
Drive from the Tennessee side (north on 129) for the best direction
Joyce Kilmer Memorial Forest nearby — old growth trees

name: Biltmore Estate
description: Largest private home in the US · 8,000 acres · winery
highlights: Biltmore House tours — scale is genuinely shocking (250 rooms)
Antler Hill Village winery tasting included with admission
Gardens designed by Frederick Law Olmsted
Asheville River Arts District galleries — incredible food scene

name: Greensboro
description: Visit Mitch · International Civil Rights Center
highlights: Visit Mitch
International Civil Rights Center & Museum — Woolworth sit-in site, powerful

name: Colonial Williamsburg
description: Living history · Jamestown · Yorktown · Shenandoah on the way home
highlights: Colonial Williamsburg living history — period actors, working trades
Jamestown Settlement — first permanent English settlement in America
Yorktown Battlefield — where the Revolution effectively ended
Shenandoah NP on the way home — Old Rag Mountain day hike (best in Mid-Atlantic)

name: Home
description: Trip complete. What a run.
highlights: Do laundry
Eat a home-cooked meal
Start planning the next one
